how_to_obtain: Remove the SD card from the drone camera after the flight and copy all image files. EXIF metadata (GPS position, camera settings, timestamps) is embedded in each file.
notes: PPK workflow uses approximate (2–5m) EXIF GPS positions; precise positions are recomputed in Stage 2 from the RINEX file using PPK. EXIF still feeds image-to-RINEX-epoch matching when MRK-equivalent CAM messages are unavailable.

how_to_obtain: Three sources in priority order: (1) CB camera library — curated parameters matched by EXIF Make+Model (highest confidence, CB_LIBRARY); (2) ODM built-in database — looked up automatically by ODM if not in CB library (ODM_DATABASE); (3) self-calibration — ODM estimates parameters from the images themselves during reconstruction (SELF_CALIBRATED). The pipeline records which path was taken via calibration_source. No pilot action required.
notes: Identity + geometry fields (L1F_CAL_001–007) feed image_capture_score and CAL_CONF inside the drone subsystem. Distortion fields (L1F_CAL_008–015) are captured here but consumed by the Processing Universe (ODM lens correction); they do not feed drone_score directly. CAL_CONF travels with the calibration file to the Processing Universe.

how_to_obtain: The GNSS receiver records raw satellite observations (.obs) and broadcast ephemerides (.nav) continuously during the flight. Downloaded from the receiver after landing — typically bundled as one logical source. PRIMARY source for PPK workflow.
notes: Raw observations are the raw material for PPK post-processing. .obs and .nav are always downloaded together and treated as one source. pre_buffer_sec and post_buffer_sec are technically computed by joining with flight timing derived from the BIN, but are surfaced as source-level fields for convenience.

how_to_obtain: Written by the flight controller to internal memory during the flight. Downloaded via Mission Planner, QGroundControl, or pymavlink directly from the FC over USB after landing. A single binary log file (typically 20–50 MB for a survey flight) that contains all of: mission waypoints (CMD), per-image shutter records (CAM), positions and altitudes (GPS/POS/CTUN), battery state (BAT), mission execution events (MISE), flight mode transitions (MODE), arm/disarm events (ARM/EV), IMU samples (IMU), vibration (VIBE), and attitude (ATT).
notes: CONSOLIDATED SOURCE — replaces the four previously-separate sources: SRC_FC_01 Mission Plan (CMD messages), SRC_FC_02 Flight Telemetry Log (GPS/POS/CTUN/BAT/MISE/MODE), SRC_FC_03 Flight Data Record (IMU/VIBE/ATT/CAM gimbal), and SRC_GNSS_02 MRK file (CAM messages, one per shutter trigger with GPS week + time-of-week + lat/lng/alt + roll/pitch/yaw). All previously-aspirational FDR fields are now live.

how_to_obtain: A small JSON form with exactly 2 fields the operator enters once per mission: planned_overlap_fwd_pct and planned_overlap_lat_pct. These are pure operator intent and are not encoded in the .BIN waypoint geometry without circular dependencies on camera calibration. All other previously user-set values (planned_altitude_m, planned_speed_ms, planned_waypoint_count, takeoff coordinates) are now sourced directly from the BIN CMD messages.
notes: New source introduced as part of the BIN-consolidation decision. Replaces the need for a separate Mission Plan file export from the GCS app. mission_pattern was originally a candidate for this form but has been removed — it fed no indicator and can be inferred from waypoint geometry if ever needed.

how_to_obtain: Pipeline issues an automated HTTPS GET using takeoff latitude/longitude (from BIN NAV_TAKEOFF CMD) and flight_start_utc (derived from BIN GPS week + time-of-week at the ARM event). Returns hourly wind speed at 10m height for the closest grid cell. Pipeline averages over the flight window to produce mean_wind_speed_ms.
notes: Sole external dependency. Fallback: if the API call fails or no connectivity, an internal proxy based on ATT roll/pitch standard deviation from the BIN is used to band wind_condition_score conservatively (calm / moderate / unknown).
